A.rationale: Stdlib loop, keyword-only base_delay, 4xx re-raised, re-raises after final attempt. Avoids import trap. Mild preamble.

B.rationale: Stdlib, retries only URLError/5xx, testable private params, no sleep after final, accurate worst-case math. Tight and complete.

C.rationale: Stdlib, 5xx-only retry, stores last exception and re-raises, no silent swallow. Correct and concise; less rationale than B.

D.rationale: Stdlib, re-raises, but retries 404/403 (HTTPError subclasses URLError); honestly flags the flaw instead of fixing it. Weaker impl.

notes: All four dodge the import trap with correct stdlib backoff that re-raises. A/B/C special-case non-transient 4xx; D retries them and only flags it. B adds clearest classification, testability hooks, accurate timing.
